product_overview.summary: Pomodoro Block (FocusFlow) is a cross-platform browser extension designed to optimize time management and minimize online distractions by combining a robust Pomodoro timer with an un-bypassable site-blocking utility built on Manifest V3.
core_value_propositions: Frictionless accessibility via system-level keyboard shortcuts.
High-performance, dark-mode-first glassmorphic user interface to mitigate cognitive load.
Unified, secure cross-browser codebase matching Web Store compliance metrics.

responsibilities: Maintain absolute persistent state of countdown timers.
Coordinate intervals and trigger state changes sequentially.
Initialize offscreen documents for predictable sound alarm playback.
Enforce Declarative Net Request rules for real-time site interception.
Connect via long-lived runtime ports to stream timer state deltas.
Render ephemeral views without locking background thread resources.
Commit localized modifications back to memory storage layers.

storage_layer.chrome_storage_local: Used for volatile runtime tracking configurations and timer state loops.
storage_layer.chrome_storage_sync: Used for persistent configuration profiles, blocklists, and device preference keys.

core_timer_engine.description: Timer countdown mechanisms must reside inside the background service worker. Closing or hiding the popup context window must not halt, desynchronize, or clear an active session sequence.
System must manage three core cycle intervals: Pomodoro (Focus Time - Default 25 min), Short Break (Default 5 min), and Long Break (Default 15 min).
Track consecutive Focus blocks. Upon reaching the user-defined interval threshold (Default: 4 cycles), the engine must automatically transition to a Long Break.
Provide explicit user entry points within the UI layout to Play, Pause, Reset, and Skip forward past active intervals.

site_blocking_engine.description: When a Pomodoro focus interval is running, network access to blacklisted domains must be instantly blocked or redirected to an internal sandbox splash file (blocked.html).
URL mapping must operate on global domain string matchers. Registering 'facebook.com' must implicitly capture subdomains (e.g., m.facebook.com) and granular paths.
Site blocking mechanisms must be strictly active only during Pomodoro sessions, unlocking instantly during Break intervals.
Provide a clean multi-line textarea input module allowing simple insertions, list purges, categorizations, and metric analysis charts reporting focus time scores.

notifications_and_audio.description: Trigger system-level messages using 'chrome.notifications' immediately upon interval completion boundaries.
Initialize a dedicated offscreen proxy layout file (offscreen.html) programmatically to support audio context manipulation within Manifest V3 rules.
Integrate an adjustable inline slider component modulating audio volume across a linear scale from 0% (Muted) to 100%.

global_shortcuts.description: Bind a global operating system shortcut command array linking directly to popup invocation: Cmd+Shift+F on macOS and Ctrl+Shift+F on Windows and Linux configurations.

main_timer_view.description: Displays the central radial countdown dial, focus phase navigation headers, playback control nodes, and persistent footer navigation targets.

blocklist_view.description: Houses the single-line site distribution textarea block, active blocklist arrays featuring interactive removal garbage nodes, and productivity metrics badges.

settings_view.description: Exposes numerical timing overrides, sequence iteration controls via plus-minus steppers, and audio control sliders.

cross_browser_compilation_strategy.build_tooling: Vite packed alongside native bundle asset splitters to cleanly isolate background artifacts.
cross_browser_compilation_strategy.compatibility_layer: Inclusion of global runtime webextension-polyfil modules to bind Chrome namespace behaviors into uniform async-await syntax structures for native Firefox integration.

non_functional_requirements.performance: Popup frame load rendering and epoch serialization sync cycles must settle in under 120ms.
non_functional_requirements.security: Strict adherence to a local operational sandbox model. Third-party structural analytics injections, external asset loads, or data transmissions are explicitly barred.
non_functional_requirements.resource_management: Service workers must aggressively handle lifetime idle timeouts, maintaining a low memory execution profile during inactive states.
